name: Irish Soda Bread (with Variant for Spotted Do
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a large bowl, mix together the dry ingredients: baking soda, baking powder, cornstarch, sugar, and salt.
3. If making the Spotted Dog variant, add raisins or sultanas to the dry mixture.
4. Pour in the buttermilk and stir until a soft dough forms. Do not overmix.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead briefly until everything comes together. Add more flour if needed to prevent sticking.
6. Shape the dough into two round loaves and flatten slightly on top.
7. Place the loaves on an ungreased baking sheet. Sprinkle with additional flour and cut diagonal slashes using a sharp paring knife.
8. Allow the loaves to rest for 10 minutes, then bake in the middle rack of the oven for 40 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through.
9. Cool on racks.
